title: Government Funding Stability Act
description: Overhauls the federal appropriations process with automatic continuing-resolution triggers, ending the recurring cycle of funding-deadline brinkmanship.
flavor: The most boring bill in Washington might also be the most useful one.

effects.description: Passive: +1 congress support/mo for remainder of term

speaker.quote: The Speaker calls it 'the rare reform both sides actually wanted — we just needed someone to force the vote.'

senate_leader.quote: The Senate Leader says it 'takes the hostage-taking out of budgeting, which is long overdue.'

opposition_leader.quote: The opposition leader admits, off the record, that this one was hard to vote against.

title: Federal Workforce Shutdown Protections Act
description: Guarantees automatic back pay, protects benefits, and bars furloughs of safety-critical federal employees during any future funding lapse.
flavor: Nobody voted to make air traffic controllers work without pay. Now nobody has to again.

effects.description: Passive: -1 unrest/mo for remainder of term

protest_leader.quote: The protest leader calls it 'proof that organizing federal workers actually moved Congress.'

senate_leader.quote: The Senate Leader warns the price tag 'sets a precedent we'll be paying for every future shutdown, real or threatened.'

title: Federal Spending Efficiency Commission Act
description: Establishes a permanent, empowered commission to review federal spending and flag waste before it becomes leverage in the next funding fight.
flavor: Everyone agrees waste is bad. Getting anyone to agree on what counts as waste is the actual fight.

effects.description: Passive: -0.02T debt/mo for remainder of term

speaker.quote: The Speaker calls it 'the kind of oversight that should've existed a decade ago.'

opposition_leader.quote: The opposition leader calls it 'a commission with a badge, looking for someone to blame.'
